prompt:
Use image 1 and image 2 only as pose-and-structure references. Do not use image 1 or image 2 as design references for outfit, robe type, cape interpretation, cloth color, hair color, or existing costume styling.

Keep only these source facts from the original image: pose, facing direction, eye direction, head angle, shoulder line, hand placement, pelvis position, leg spacing, knee height, calf width, boot placement, and overall body placement inside the frame. Preserve the full visible body from hair to boots. Keep the exact composition, empty padding, and center alignment of the original source frame. Do not redesign the animation anatomy. Push the overall read toward a more heroic near-five-head SD proportion, but do that within the original runtime silhouette envelope instead of stretching or rescaling the character arbitrarily.

If the original image looks like a cape, trailing cloth, or a lighter-color outfit, ignore that completely. Preserve only the body pose and structural placement. The new outfit and colors must come only from the Dungeon Mage art direction described below.

Restyle only the character design details for Dungeon Mage. This is the player character: a handsome teenage academy mage who entered the labyrinth for his graduation exam. He should read as a trained elite student mage, kind and intelligent, with a distinctly sharp, cool, and disciplined presence. Keep him youthful and noble, not dark or villainous. His body should read as lean and athletic, closer to a 174 cm / 64 kg trained student mage, slim rather than bulky, with light muscle definition rather than a heavy torso.

Push the face away from a round cute chibi read. Give him a cleaner narrower jaw line, less rounded cheeks, a more defined nose bridge, a restrained mouth shape, and long sharp eyes with a strong upper-eyelid line. The brow line should look focused and intelligent. He should still look like a good-natured student mage, but with an intense, composed, elite expression. Add a very slight mischievous confidence to the face, like a talented boy who knows he can handle himself, but do not let the expression become goofy, smug, or comedic. The expression should balance sharp concentration, calm self-possession, and a faint playful edge. Do not make the eyes overly large, circular, or innocent-looking. Avoid a smiling or soft baby-faced expression.

The pupils must always face in the same direction as the torso and body orientation. If the body faces right, the pupils and eye direction must also face right. Keep the eye direction, face direction, and body-facing read locked together.

His hair should be a warm golden-orange blond with a noticeable orange cast inside the blond range. Keep it short, layered, and sharp, with strong strand separation and angular silhouette breaks. Use a half-swept hairstyle: roughly half of the hair mass is swept back, while the remaining front and side sections frame the face with sharper layered bangs near the eyes. The hair can feel lively and slightly unruly while still staying academy-student appropriate. Do not copy any specific franchise hairstyle, signature face marking, headband, or iconic costume element.

The costume should be dominated by a long academy robe and remain readable at small gameplay scale. Keep the robe long, dignified, and student-academy oriented. The robe should be almost black but still clearly a very dark blue. Use only a small amount of gold ornament: restrained gold trim, a small academy crest detail, and minimal gold accents at select edges. Do not overdecorate the robe with gold accessories.

This is a robe, not a cape. Do not let it read as a cape thrown behind the body. Do not let it flare or flutter backward in idle. The garment should wrap most of the front, back, and sides of the character like a true robe. The robe should remain mostly closed across the torso, except for a very narrow vertical opening at the upper center chest around the sternum area. Through that narrow opening, show only a limited glimpse of a dark vest in a robe-matching tone, a white shirt, and a red tie. The robe must remain the dominant outer silhouette.

Do not add a belt, waist sash, waist strap, or any strong waist-cinching accessory to the robe. The robe should fall cleanly from the shoulders and torso instead of being tightly gathered at the waist. Keep the robe long while still preserving the original lower-body readability from image 1 and image 2. Use a front-split or center-slit lower structure so the pelvis, legs, boots, and original idle lower-body silhouette remain readable. Do not let the robe collapse into one solid mass that hides the legs. Preserve the original robe hem placement logic and the visible lower-body silhouette from the source keyframe.

Use robe folds that feel directional, angular, disciplined, and stable rather than rounded, puffy, or wind-blown. The sleeves may be broad and structured, with restrained gold-trimmed cuffs, but they should still read as a student mage robe rather than a professor or royal court outfit. Shoes should be dark leather and harmonize with the robe palette. No gloves. The crossbody bag may remain barely visible because it sits mostly behind the body.

For this idle keyframe, the wand does not need to be visible in the hands. Keep the original hand placement and gameplay silhouette stable even when the wand is hidden from view.

Style: pixel-friendly 2D fantasy action RPG character, same visual family as Dungeon Mage monsters but cleaner, more disciplined, and more heroic. Lighting and mood: bright readable character treatment over subdued dungeon gameplay backgrounds. Background handling: transparent background, isolated single character keyframe. Important: preserve the original keyframe composition exactly and do not redesign the anatomy or pose.
